gluten_free.note: Plain 'modified starch' is ambiguous (can be gluten-free corn/potato starch) — EU labelling requires the specific source to be named if it is a gluten grain.

lactose_free.note: Caseinate and whey are dairy-derived even when the word 'milk' does not appear.

vegan.note: L-cysteine (E920) used as a dough conditioner is frequently derived from duck/human feathers or synthesised; check the manufacturer if it matters to you.

vegetarian.note: Rennet in cheese and gelatine in gummy sweets/marshmallows are the most commonly missed non-vegetarian ingredients.

nut_allergy.note: Praline, marzipan and nougat are common hidden sources of tree nuts in confectionery.

peanut_allergy.note: 'Arachis oil' and 'groundnut oil' are both peanut oil under different names.

halal.note: Gelatine and enzymes (rennet, L-cysteine) require certified halal sourcing to confirm suitability.

kosher.note: Mixing meat and dairy derivatives in the same product is also a kosher concern beyond individual ingredients.

soy_allergy.note: E322 (lecithin) is very frequently soy-derived unless labelled 'sunflower lecithin'.

egg_allergy.note: Lysozyme (E1105), sometimes used in cheese, is egg-derived.
